title: LRU Cache
pattern: Design â€” Hashmap + Doubly Linked List
coreIdea: Design a cache with O(1) get and put that evicts the least recently used item when capacity is exceeded, achieved by combining a hashmap (for O(1) lookup) with a doubly linked list (for O(1) reordering to track recency).
recognitionCues: asks to design a cache with capacity and eviction policy
requires O(1) for both get and put operations
approach: Maintain a hashmap from key to node, and a doubly linked list ordered by recency (most recent at head/tail). On get, move the accessed node to the most-recent end. On put, if key exists update and move to most-recent end; if at capacity, remove the least-recently-used node (opposite end) before inserting the new one.
